patch.insert_text: She reports wooziness only when she is hungry.
added_claims: Patient reports wooziness (lightheadedness) only when hungry

reasons: Evidence 'Just the wooziness if I am hungry' directly supports the claim of hunger-related wooziness
Note mentions fatigue and morning queasiness but not wooziness when hungry, so not duplicated
Patient-reported symptom belongs in Subjective section

patch.insert_text: She denies uncontrolled vomiting, dizziness, or fainting.

reasons: The claim 'denies dizziness' contradicts the evidence, in which the patient reports 'wooziness if I am hungry' (i.e., she does experience dizziness when hungry).
'Uncontrolled vomiting' and 'fainting' may be covered by 'nothing like that,' but the specific symptoms being denied are not clearly established by the vague span.
Content is new and not duplicated in the note.
Subjective is the correct section for patient-reported symptom denials.

patch.insert_text: She denies uncontrolled vomiting or fainting, noting only mild wooziness when hungry.

reasons: Only the 'wooziness when hungry' claim is directly supported; the evidence span 'No, nothing like that' is ambiguous and does not explicitly name uncontrolled vomiting or fainting, so those two specific denials are inferred/unsupported.
Content (fainting, uncontrolled vomiting, wooziness) is not already documented in the note.
Symptom denials/reports correctly belong in the Subjective section.
